risks: No Network Security Groups (NSGs) defined for subnets or NICs — VMs are reachable without network-level filtering rules
No monitoring or alerting resources (Log Analytics workspace, Azure Monitor alerts) for ASR replication health or VM metrics
Recovery plan references runbook 'runbook-dns-failover-prod-001' in endGroupActions but does not declare it as an explicit dependency, risking deployment ordering issues
No on-demand capacity reservation in West US to guarantee VM compute availability during a regional disaster
VMs do not have boot diagnostics enabled, which limits post-failover troubleshooting capability
Storage account 'stdrprimarycache001' does not disable local (shared key) authentication — best practice is to use managed identity and disable key-based access
Recovery Services vault has publicNetworkAccess set to Enabled — consider private endpoints for enhanced security
correctionsRecommended: Add NSGs for each subnet (snet-vms-prod-eastus-001, snet-vms-prod-westus-001) with appropriate inbound/outbound rules restricting traffic to required ports only
Add a Log Analytics workspace and configure diagnostic settings on the Recovery Services vault, VMs, and Automation Account for centralized monitoring
Add 'runbook-dns-failover-prod-001' to the dependencies array of 'asr-recovery-plan-prod-001' to ensure correct deployment ordering
Consider adding on-demand capacity reservations in the West US recovery region to guarantee VM SKU availability during widespread outages
Enable boot diagnostics on both VMs using a diagnostics storage account or managed storage for post-failover troubleshooting
Set 'allowSharedKeyAccess: false' on the cache storage account and use managed identity for ASR cache access
Add resource tags (environment, application, costCenter) across all resources for operational governance
